issue_title: "Could not copy" error when the project reference is shared
issue_body:
# Issue Title

"Could not copy" error when the project reference is shared between several projects

# General

Suppose that two projects of  the solution share one reference.

 <ProjectReference Include="..\........src\SomeSharedLibrary.csproj"  />

The first project  (the service) is running in console window with **dotnet run** , the other app is the client for this service and runs (_usually, but indeed does not matter_) from Visual Studio.

Whenever you stop the client project and run it again it will fail to compile with annoying error

> Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State
> Error	MSB3021	Unable to copy file "D:\projects\....\bin\Debug\netcoreapp1.1\....dll" to "bin\Debug\netcoreapp2.0\X.....". The process cannot access the file 'bin\Debug\netcoreapp2.0\...dll' because it is being used by another process.	.....	C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2017\Community\MSBuild\15.0\Bin\Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets	4277	

It happens regardless if you have made any changes or not, moreover, even though you stop and restart the service in console windows - it does not help. The error is still occur. 

Formerly I was able at least  to restart the service to get rid of error, without closing the window, Now it became impossible, I have to close window, then reopen it and re-run **dotnet run**. 

I even do not dream about the normal scenario - e.g when I make changes to the  client project (_not touching Shared library - it gets changed very seldom_),  and I am able to run it again without stopping the service.

Tried **PrivateAssets="All"** didn't help, shared files are still get locked.

Therefore I have to terminate the service, then close the window, then reopen the window and run it again, run the client, reattach to service process (if I need to debug) - just to run the client app again, even without any changes in it. Nightmare !

issue_title: `dotnet new --uninstall` not working for folder templates
issue_body:
# Unable to uninstall folder-based templates

This could be an issue with the `dotnet net --uninstall` (or `dotnet new -u`) command, or just an issue with [uninstall documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/custom-templates#to-uninstall-a-template-from-a-file-system-directory), but it doesn't seem possible to uninstall a template via a folder template.

See also this [Stack Overflow question](https://stackoverflow.com/q/45865134/48700).

# General

## Steps

1. Create template folder (**.template.config** folder with a **template.json** file)

    I have confirmed it with this JSON config.

    ```json
    {
        "$schema": "http://json.schemastore.org/template",
        "author": "Some Author",
        "classifications": [ "Some", "Various", "Search Keywords" ],
        "identity": "Some.Identity",
        "shortName": "name-used-in-dotnetnew",
        "name": "Some Template Name"
    }
    ```

2. [Install template](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/custom-templates#to-install-a-template-from-a-file-system-directory)

    ```bash
    dotnet new --install ./some-template-folder/
    ```

3. [optional] Use newly-installed template

    ```bash
    The template "Some Template Name" was created successfully.
    ```

4. Attempt to [uninstall template](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/custom-templates#to-uninstall-a-template-from-a-file-system-directory)

    ```bash
    dotnet new --uninstall ./some-template-folder/
    ```

    Instead of uninstalling, I keep getting this error message.

    > `Could not find something to uninstall called '.\some-template-folder\'.`

This happened with .NET Core SDK v1.1.0 and v2.0.0.

Remove "-preview" from client and repository name

issue_title: Disable automatic -0001 suffixes in cert storage
issue_body:
# Scenario

I start with generating a single cert for "example.com, www.example.com, test.example.com", this is stored in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example.com`

Later I generate a new cert with "example.com, www.example.com"
This will be stored in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example.com-0001`

# Problems

At this stage it is unclear whether the user intended to remove `test.example.com` or to generate a new cert.

The risks are that one might not notice the suffix and assume that the new certs are in the original location.
I'm not suggesting silently replacing the cert in the original location.

**The location of the generated certs should be known before running the client.**

# Suggestion

With this issue I suggest removal of the automatic suffix generation.

The algorithm that triggers the use of prefix should still be in use but once triggered I suggest the following approach.

When triggered an error will occur.
The error message will instruct that one of the following approaches is required.

1. Indicate that you want to modify the domains in the current cert. This feature is suggested in #2071 
2. Choose a new alias for the storage using an option like `--alias`

With option 2 the use of `--alias example2` would store the certs in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example2`

These two options can work independently and combined.

# Examples

If your intention was to remove `test.example.com` then you use 1

If you intention was to generate another different cert then use 2

If you want to further modify the domain list for the cert in the new location you must use both 1 and 2.

issue_title: Change link to PGP key server
issue_body:
The pgp.mit.edu keyserver has been having a lot of 503 errors lately, which has been causing the `tox -e docs` builds to fail their dead links check.

It might be a good idea to switch that over to a new endpoint that is more stable, or add a mirror.

I'm also thinking that maybe the link error checker might need to be an "allowed-fail" on PRs. It's super useful in preventing linkrot, but it's causing a lot of false alarm Travis build failures.

issue_title: Update PYPI
metadata.search returns keys in new-to-old order
issue_body:
```
import json
import imagery
import descarteslabs as dl
dlk = dl.raster.dlkey_from_latlon(31.42417852, -96.24699805, 30., 512, 0)

# get scenes using imagery.search
scenes1 = imagery.search(satellite='L8', shape=json.dumps(dlk['geometry']), start_time='2014-01-01', end_time='2014-03-01')

# get scenes using metadata.search
scenes2 = dl.metadata.search(const_id=['L8'], geom=json.dumps(dlk['geometry']), start_time='2014-01-01', end_time='2014-03-01')
```

`imagery.search` returns keys in old-to-new order.
```
[u'meta_LC80260382014015_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014031_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014047_v1']
```


`metadata.search` returns keys in new-to-old order.
```
[u'meta_LC80260382014047_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014031_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014015_v1']
```

IMO, old-to-new order is preferable, because then `.raster(multiple_scenes)` will make a mosaic image with the most recent imagery on top.

Now, things get more complicated when you pass multiple `const_id`s to `metadata.search`.  The results come back grouped by `const_id`, and are sorted new-to-old within each `const_id`.  For example, searching L7 and L8 yields:

```
[u'meta_LE70260382014055_v1',
 u'meta_LE70260382014039_v1',
 u'meta_LE70260382014023_v1',
 u'meta_LE70260382014007_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014047_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014031_v1',
 u'meta_LC80260382014015_v1']
```

I don't know how difficult this would be to implement, but I propose that we return the scenes with a global ordering based explicitly on the scene acquisition time, going from old-to-new.  In that case, `.raster(multiple_scenes)` would make a mosaic image with the most recent imagery on top.

It seems that the `missing` value should be acquired before validation for this to work as expected.  Or is this user error?

Thanks!
-chad

issue_title: Deprecate allow_null parameter of fields.Nested?
Allow passing original value to validators of a specific field
issue_body:
## Description

I want to validate and deserialize an `DateTime` file according to [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format (`YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss+tz_hh:tz_mm`), so this is my code:
```python
def validate_iso8601_datetime(value):
    pattern = (
        r'^(?P<year>[0-9]{4})-(?P<month>1[0-2]|0[1-9])-(?P<day>3[01]|0[1-9]|[12][0-9])'
        'T(?P<hour>2[0-3]|[01][0-9]):(?P<minute>[0-5][0-9]):(?P<second>[0-5][0-9])'
        '\+(?P<tz_hh>[0-9]{2}):(?P<tz_mm>[0-9]{2})$'
    )
    match = re.match(pattern, value)
    if not match:
        raise ValidationError('{} is not match the ISO8601 format.'.format(value))

class ReservationSchema(Schema):
    working_date = fields.DateTime(format="iso", validate=validate_iso8601_datetime)

data = {
    "working_date": "2015-06-07 22:00:00+07:00"
}
result, errors = ReservationSchema().load(data)

==> TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object
```
We have the `TypeError` exception because `value` passed to `validate_iso8601_datetime()` function is `datetime` type (not a `string` type).
So I think we should support a way to pass original data to validator function (`validate` param in field declaration)

## Proposed API
Allow passing original data to validator function:
```python
Field(..., validate=validate_function, pass_original=False)
```

What do you guys think about this proposal?

issue_title: Upgrade project to use CircleCI 2.0
InverseLayer cannot invert MergeLayer
issue_body:
The `InverseLayer` currently assumes the layer to be inverted has a single incoming layer:
https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/b6c3e5c/lasagne/layers/special.py#L344

The `InverseLayer` should get an optional third argument specifying the input to backpropagate to. This would allow inverting a layer of multiple inputs, and it would even allow inverting multiple layers at once. If omitted, it should use `layer.input_layer` as before.

For the name of the additional argument, we could use `backprop_to`, or `wrt` / `with_respect_to`. The docstring should be rewritten anyway (https://groups.google.com/d/topic/lasagne-users/pn6fIglr2XU/discussion), so `wrt` would make sense.

issue_title: Got 'AssertionError: ('Rebroadcast needs bool for new broadcast pattern. Got ', False)' after adding a ReshapeLayer
issue_body:
```python
import lasagne
import lasagne.layers as layers
from lasagne.objectives import squared_error, aggregate
from lasagne import regularization
import theano.tensor as T
from theano import function


def objective(output_layer,
              regularize_layers,
              target,
              loss_function=squared_error,
              aggregate=aggregate,
              deterministic=False,
              l1=0,
              l2=0,
              tv=0):
    network_output = layers.get_output(
        output_layer, deterministic=deterministic)
    loss = aggregate(loss_function(network_output, target))
    for layer in regularize_layers:
        if l1:
            loss +=\
                regularization.regularize_layer_params(layer,
                                                       regularization.l1) * l1
        if l2:
            loss +=\
                regularization.regularize_layer_params(layer,
                                                       regularization.l2) * l2
    if tv:
        loss += T.mean(T.abs_(network_output[:, 1:] -
                              network_output[:, :-1]))*tv
    return loss


filter_len = 121
pooling_size = 40
t1 = 7
t2 = 5
s_num = 19
input_size = pooling_size * (t2 + t1 - 1) + filter_len - 1
x = T.tensor4()
l_in = layers.InputLayer((None, 62, 1, input_size), input_var=x)
l_sf = layers.NINLayer(l_in, num_units=s_num,
                       b=None, nonlinearity=None)
l_ds = layers.DimshuffleLayer(l_sf, (0, 2, 1, 3))
l_conv1 = layers.Conv2DLayer(l_ds, num_filters=3, filter_size=(1, filter_len),
                             b=None,
                             nonlinearity=None)
l_pool = layers.FeaturePoolLayer(l_conv1, 40, 3,
                                 lambda x, axis: T.log(T.sum(x**2, axis)))
l_conv2 = layers.Conv2DLayer(l_pool, num_filters=1, filter_size=(s_num, t1),
                             nonlinearity=None)
l_conv3 = layers.Conv2DLayer(l_pool, num_filters=1, filter_size=(s_num, t1),
                             nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.sigmoid)
l_merge = layers.ElemwiseMergeLayer([l_conv2, l_conv3], T.mul)
l_rs = layers.ReshapeLayer(l_merge, ([0], -1))
regularize_layers = [l_conv2, l_conv3]
t = T.matrix()
params = layers.get_all_params(l_rs, trainable=True)
loss = objective(l_rs, regularize_layers, t)
updates = lasagne.updates.sgd(loss, params, learning_rate=1e-4)
train_fn = function([x, t], loss, updates=updates)
# # # # # # # # #
# t = T.tensor4()
# params = layers.get_all_params(l_merge, trainable=True)
# loss = objective(l_merge, regularize_layers, t)
# updates = lasagne.updates.sgd(loss, params, learning_rate=1e-4)
# train_fn = function([x, t], loss, updates=updates)
```
I got `AssertionError: ('Rebroadcast needs bool for new broadcast pattern. Got ', False)` running this script, but if I remove the ReshapeLayer and calculate updates directly using MergeLayer, there's no error.

issue_title: Use name not tags for unique cell objects
issue_body:
In 

https://github.com/nteract/papermill/blob/7b717e763528915dd77865d418011d86f54b528c/papermill/execute.py#L157-L163

it makes the assumption that only one cell will have any parameters being defined in the notebook and raises an error if this is not the case.

We have `name` as a cell-level metadata field in the [nbformat spec](http://nbformat.readthedocs.io/en/latest/format_description.html#cell-metadata) that specifically requires values to be unique across the notebook. If you want to maintain this uniqueness qualifier it would be cleaner to require setting the name on a cell rather than a tag.

If the only reason to not do this is that the UI for setting a `name` vs. `tags` is more inconvenient, that suggests we have a frontend UI issue. In that case… using tags is just a compromise made for the current state of front-ends and should probably be deprecated as soon as possible in favour of setting `name` properly.

issue_title: TypeError: comparing strings with non-ASCII characters is not supported
issue_body:
at https://github.com/pyotp/pyotp/blob/8e490934a6d9ef27fdbbb7ec94c7ee9cf83cba9d/src/pyotp/utils.py#L69.

`hmac.compare_digest` does not support strings which contain non-ASCII characters.

User typed codes would typically be ASCII, but sometimes with non-ASCII. In particular, Japanese people often use FULLWIDTH digits (`\uff10`[０]  - `\uff19`[９]) instead of ASCII digits.

So, to prevent above exception, pyotp should normalize codes based on Unicode before pass to `hmac.compare_digest` or simply ignore `TypeError`.

issue_title: Use git lfs
issue_body:
This repo is getting pretty big, and will only get bigger as we  add more workflows with chgcars, etc. I suggest we move to using git lfs for faster clones and pulls. It is pretty transparent, and the only requirement is that the developers have to install git lfs first. I have already implemented lfs support with a custom image for testing.

Pymatgen has already moved to a git lfs system. The impetus is even stronger for atomate.

issue_title: enhancement to FunctionFeaturizer
issue_body:
One potential new capability of the FunctionFeaturizer (or derivative of it) would be a ``fit()`` type method that would try a bunch of functions for a single variable and relate it to an output variable. Then, pick the functional form that best correlates with the output as your descriptor.

e.g., I have a descriptor like "avg mass" and want to correlate this with the output "thermal conductivity".

The FunctionFeaturizer would try things like:
- square of avg mass
- exp^(avg_mass)
- exp^(-avg_mass)
- 1/(avg_mass)
- ln(avg_mass)

And in the end, instead of creating a dozen new features, will just retain the one functional form that best correlates with the output. Thus there will only be a single feature for avg_mass (not a dozen), but it might have a different functional form.

issue_title: graphene-django expose form field choices as enum
issue_body:
Actually, the choice form field is converted to a graphene string when the best solution is an enum field. 
I have a posible solution for this. Implementing a converter function like `convert_django_field_with_choices` with a few changes we can extract the choices an export  enum field.
  ```python 
def convert_django_form_field_with_choices(field):
    """
    Helper method to convert a field to graphene Field type.
    :param field: form field to convert to
    :return: graphene Field
    """
    choices = getattr(field, 'choices', None)

    if choices:
        name = to_camel_case(field.label)  # use the field label as identifier 
        choices = list(get_choices(choices))
        named_choices = [(c[0], c[1]) for c in choices]
        named_choices_descriptions = {c[0]: c[2] for c in choices}

        class EnumWithDescriptionsType(object):
            """Enum type definition"""

            @property
            def description(self):
                """Return field description"""

                return named_choices_descriptions[self.name]

        enum = Enum(name, list(named_choices), type=EnumWithDescriptionsType)
        return enum(description=field.help_text, required=field.required)  
    return convert_form_field(field)

```
Then a few changes on the function `field_for_fom`.

```python 
def fields_for_form(form, only_fields, exclude_fields):
    """
    Helper method for extract form fields and convert it to graphene types Fields
    :param form: form instance
    :param only_fields: form only_fields
    :param exclude_fields: form exclude_fields
    :return: OrderedDict with converted fields
    """
    fields = OrderedDict()
    for name, field in form.fields.items():
        is_not_in_only = only_fields and name not in only_fields
        is_excluded = (
            name
            in exclude_fields  # or
            # name in already_created_fields
        )

        if is_not_in_only or is_excluded:
            continue

        if field.label is None:   # ensure the label is given or set the field name 
            field.label = name

        fields[name] = convert_django_field_with_choices(field) # call the implemented function
    return fields
```

Let's take for example the form.
```python
class MyForm(forms.Form):
    STATUS_CHOICES = (
        (PENDING, _('Pending status')),
        (COMPLETED, _('Completed status'))
    )
    status = forms.ChoiceField(choices=STATUS_CHOICES)
.....
```
The input type of the status field on the mutation is now an enum
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16706366/44685386-56aeca00-aa19-11e8-9c86-04ebbb982b83.png)

I will be glad to create a pull request if it needed.

issue_title: [spinel-py] Remove TunInterface dependency from codec.py
issue_body:
Here's the use case: I'd like to use the spinel package on Windows to control an NCP. To make this possible I'd like to:

- Remove the dependency on TunInterface from codec.py. It looks to me that it's only there to be accessible for `if_up` and `if_down` methods. These are called from `spinel-cli.py`. How about making the TunInterface a member object of `SpinelCliCmd` class and removing self.tun_if member from `WpanApi` class.
- A side effect of the change above is that we can get rid of nodeid from WpanApi constructor. Which, I think, is desired since nodeId is simulator specific whereas the WpanApi isn't
- All code which refers to the TunInterface would be guarded to be available only on Posix.

Are there any objections to these changes?

issue_title: customized objective function, getting error messages
issue_body:
Hi, I am new to tensorflow and tflearn. I would like to define my own cost function. I have tried both methods mentioned in [issue #72 ](https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/issues/72) but in both cases I get error messages. In the first case, the error message is:

from .config import _EPSILON, _FLOATX
ValueError: Attempted relative import in non-package

and in the second case:

"./tflearn/layers/estimator.py", line 135, in regression
exit()
NameError: global name 'exit' is not defined

I have spent some time trying to fix this, to no avail. Any help would be highly appreciated.

#########################################################################
Below is my code for the cost function:

```python
def deep_clustering(y_pred,y_true):
   
    with tf.name_scope("DeepClustering"):            
        n1,n2,c=y_true.shape
        N=n1*n2
        k=int(y_pred.shape[0]/N)
        
        Yc=tf.reshape(y_true,[N,c])   # target labels 
        Vk=tf.reshape(y_pred,[N,k])   # estimated embeddings
        
        YtY=tf.matmul(Yc.T,Yc)
        VtV=tf.matmul(Vk.T,Vk)  
        VtY=tf.matmul(Vk.T,Yc) 
        
        norm2Y_F=tf.trace(tf.matmul(YtY,YtY.T)) # squred Frobenius norm of YtY
        norm2V_F=tf.trace(tf.matmul(VtV,VtV.T)) # squared Frobenius norm of VtV
        norm2VY_F = tf.trace(tf.matmul(VtY,VtY.T)) # squared Frobenius norm of VtY
        
        loss = norm2Y_F - 2*norm2VY_F + norm2V_F        
    return loss
```

issue_title: mismatch in vec and bin files in french pretrained vector
issue_body:
For all other pretrained vector models, `vocab_size` obtained from `vec` file is equal to the `size` and `nwords` obtained from `bin` file. ( this [line](https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/blob/master/src/dictionary.cc#L373))

But for `wiki.fr` , `vocab_size` is `1152449`, `size` is `1152450`, and `nwords` is `1152449`. Further analysing, this additional `vocab_word` is `u'__label__'` which is not present in `vec` file, or any other pretrained vector model.

This doesn't cause any bug in fastText code, but I found it little unusual. It would be really helpful, if somebody could provide an insight or explanation behind this.

issue_title: Mention `less -S` alongside csvlook
issue_body:
In the tutorial for [csvlook](http://csvkit.readthedocs.org/en/0.9.1/tutorial/1_getting_started.html#csvlook-data-periscope), the docs mention:

> Now at first the output of csvlook isn’t going to appear very promising. You’ll see a mess of data, pipe character and dashes. That’s because this dataset has many columns and they won’t all fit in the terminal at once. To fix this we need to learn how to reduce our dataset before we look at it.

An easy way to preview this mess of data, pipes, etc. is to pipe it to `less -S`, which (a) renders the file from the top allowing you to scroll down, and (b) the `-S` argument also lets you scroll horizontally.

Piping `csvlook` to `less -S` has been a game changer for me.

I was about to make a pull request to put this in the docs, but it kind of breaks the flow of the tutorial (piping isn't mentioned yet, and there's currently a nice lead-in to `csvcut`). Do you have any preference on where this tidbit might live?

issue_title: Nginx plugin does not see hosts starting with dot or new line
Fully check for Nginx address equality
issue_body: `"0.0.0.0"`, `"*"`, and `""` are equivalent to Nginx but not to the parser. This will manifest as a failure to correctly label server blocks as `ssl`, or anywhere else address equality is tested. This should probably be fixed in the Nginx `Addr` object when testing equality.

Badges notification mails are not sent because the badges signals ([`on_badge_added`](https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/master/udata/core/badges/signals.py#L9) and [`on_badge_removed`](https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/master/udata/core/badges/signals.py#L9) are never dispatched.

## Context
#885 and #887  highlighted the fact that badges metrics were not in use because signals are never dispatched.
Notifications mail are connected on the `on_badge_added` and so are never sent too.

## Possible Implementation
Dispatch the signals were they should be (on badges addition and removal)

## Your Environment
udata 1.0.x

The information would be `key: value` pairs in the format of

```
status:  completed
start: time
end: time
max_cpu: xxx
```

Assuming that the kernel would send such information through the `update-task` message, what would be the best way to present them?

I am thinking of some icon like:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9889312/23918907/c2ce28d8-08c2-11e7-9572-ff9dfe506540.png)

but there can be better and easier method. Note that the task list is currently implemented as simple tables so a collapsible table (display header by default, click to expand to see the whole table) would also work.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9889312/23919006/16f24e62-08c3-11e7-862f-5cb299e48304.png)

I suppose something like a pop-up window would also work.

issue_title: Behavior of `paired_with` when used in conjunction with `group_by`
issue_body:
I'm trying to write a mixed-style SoS but got stuck at what boils down to this MWE:

```
input_files = ['{}.txt'.format(x) for x in range(5,9)]
data = [('1.txt', '2.txt'), ('3.txt', '4.txt')]

[1]
output: input_files
run:
 touch ${output}

[2]
input: input_files, group_by = 2, paired_with = "data"
print("${_input}")
print("${_data}")
```

I expect this script to work because it feels natural that when used with `group_by` the `paired_with` option should apply to input groups. However it does not work. In this case I can potentially "flat" `data` and get it work, but there may be cases when each group of `data` has different number of elements than each group of `input`. Should we change the behavior of `paired_with` for this case?

```
if condition:
```

```
sh: skip=not condition
   script
```
so that we can use script format of actions even under conditions.

issue_title: Add functionality to mute specific receivers of signals (not just all signals)
issue_body:
#### The problem
As described at https://factoryboy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/orms.html#disabling-signals, it is possible to mute all signals of a certain type using the `factory.django.mute_signals` decorator. In my use case, hwoever, I have a specific receiver of `post_save` signals which calls an external API, and I would like to mute only this receiver (and not all `post_save` signals). 

#### Proposed solution
We could write a `mute_receivers` decorator/context manager along the lines of [`mute_signals`](https://github.com/FactoryBoy/factory_boy/blob/2d735767b7f3e1f9adfc3f14c28eeef7acbf6e5a/factory/django.py#L256) which does this.

issue_title: Factory Boy returning different type_id for Generic Foreign Key
issue_body:
I have a model like the following:
```python
class MyModel(TimeStampedModel):
    entity_type = models.ForeignKey(ContentType)
    entity_id = models.PositiveIntegerField(db_index=True)
    entity = GenericForeignKey('entity_type', 'entity_id')
    other_field = .....
```

Now I create entry in the table like following:
```python
my_object = SomeModel.objects.create()
MyModel.objects.create(entity=my_object)
```

And I query MyModel like:
```python
MyModel.objects.filter(entity_id=my_object.id,
    entity_type_id=ContentType.objects.get_for_model(SomeModel).id)
```

The select query does not return anything.
By logging I found out that while running tests in factory boy, the entity_type_id that gets created while inserting the object is different than what is returned by ContentType.objects.get_for_model(SomeModel).id

Please suggest what I can do to get the test done.

issue_title: mail-parser fails to install depending on locale
issue_body:
mail-parser currently fails to install depending on system locale (`LC_CTYPE`), like so:

```
$ LC_CTYPE=C pip install mail-parser --user
Collecting mail-parser
  Using cached https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/10/fa/988a3fe204d7cca689d75e623adf45a2f57c43e55603b73b7ffaf092a7e0/mail-parser-3.3.2.tar.gz

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/tmp/pip-install-k3_u6ooo/mail-parser/setup.py", line 28, in <module>
        long_description = f.read()
      File "/usr/lib/python3.6/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
        return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
    UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 6050: ordinal not in range(128)
    
    ----------------------------------------
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-install-k3_u6ooo/mail-parser/
```

If an UTF-8 locale is provided, it installs ok:

```
$ LC_CTYPE=en_US.utf8 pip install mail-parser --user
Collecting mail-parser
  Using cached https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/10/fa/988a3fe204d7cca689d75e623adf45a2f57c43e55603b73b7ffaf092a7e0/mail-parser-3.3.2.tar.gz
Requirement already satisfied: ipaddress in ./.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages (from mail-parser) (1.0.22)
Requirement already satisfied: simplejson in ./.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages (from mail-parser) (3.15.0)

Installing collected packages: mail-parser

Successfully installed mail-parser-3.3.2
```

This means that mail-parser will fail to install on a fresh [docker ubuntu 18.04 image](https://hub.docker.com/r/_/ubuntu) as no locale is set by default.  (This was how I discovered this bug.)


issue_title: Option to use hostnames instead of explicit ip addresses
issue_body:
Can we start ray using hostnames for the redis_address instead of an explicit IP?
This might be difficult to do correctly on the master node.
However, it should be easy on the worker node, and really only matters for the worker nodes connecting to the master.

Currently, plasma manager complains if a hostname is supplied instead of an IP.
The issue is [parse_ip_addr](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc#L1749) is returning -1 since the following [regex](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/src/common/net.cc#L11) doesn't capture hostnames.

A simple quick fix I found was changing "%15[0-9.]:%5[0-9]" to "%[^:]:%5[0-9]".
There's probably a more careful way to do this using strtok.

issue_title: BBC iPlayer Live TV
issue_body:
When streaming live channels from iplayer e.g. http://www.bbc.co.uk/iplayer/live/bbcone (geoip restricted to UK) it defaults to 960x540 25fps video.

```
Streamlink:
[cli][info] Available streams: 544k_alt, 1700k_alt, 1700k_alt2, 544k_alt2, 352k_alt2, 942k_alt2, 540p_alt, 288p_alt, 942k_alt, 540p_alt2, 396p_alt2, 288p_alt2, 396p_alt, 352k_alt, 352k (worst), 544k, 288p, 942k, 396p, 540p, 1700k (best)
[cli][info] Opening stream: 1700k (hds)
```

get_iplayer had a streaming option which allows streaming up to 720p 50fps [info](https://github.com/get-iplayer/get_iplayer/wiki/modesref#tv-modes). Unfortunately the developer removed the streaming feature from new versions. 

```
get_iplayer:
INFO: hvfhd1,hvfhd2,hvfhd3,hvfhd4,hvfsd1,hvfsd2,hvfsd3,hvfsd4,hvfvhigh1,hvfvhigh2,hvfvhigh3,hvfvhigh4,hvfhigh1,hvfhigh2,hvfhigh3,hvfhigh4,hvfstd1,hvfstd2,hvfstd3,hvfstd4,hvflow1,hvflow2,hvflow3,hvflow4 modes will be tried for version default
INFO: Trying hvfhd1 mode to stream livetv: BBC One - live

 (+) Video --vid=1 (h264 1280x720 50.000fps)
 (+) Audio --aid=1 (aac 2ch 48000Hz)
```

Is it possible to force mpegdash/hls 720p 50fps stream with streamlink or would a new feature be needed to enable it?

issue_title: Runoff app
High memory usage with large S3 objects
issue_body:
In my project, we need to test that uploads and downloads of large (> 1GB) files work, and we’re using LocalStack to emulate S3 for our local tests. On startup, our script uploads 3GB of files (most small, a few large) into LocalStack S3, and after that its memory usage balloons:

```
CONTAINER ID  NAME        CPU %  MEM USAGE / LIMIT    MEM %   NET I/O          BLOCK I/O       PIDS
1ce5b092620d  localstack  0.71%  6.178GiB / 11.71GiB  52.75%  3.58GB / 16.3MB  102kB / 8.19kB  14
```

I have my Docker for Mac configured to allow up to 12GB used by containers, and my machine has 64GB of RAM, so it works on my machine; but my colleagues with only 16GB of RAM and 8GB allocated to Docker report Docker crashing when this starts up.

Are these uploaded S3 objects simply stored in memory? Are they never written to disk? Could they perhaps be written to disk somewhere, in a path that I can mount as a volume?

issue_title: Make checksumming of downloaded products the default behavior

It is a good practice to verify the integrity of downloaded products and it makes sense to do it by default.
So, let's set `checksum` to `True` in `download()` and `download_all()` and change `--md5` to `--no-md5` in the CLI.
This is a backwards-incompatible change, so we probably want to do implement it in a release that bundles other such changes.

issue_title: SUP-0002 - Queries Should Feel Like Variables
issue_body:
# SUP-0002
## Queries Should Feel Like Variables

Consider: 

`s_balance = balances.select().where(balances.address == sender).run()`

If we want to update this balance, we have to rerun a new query against the database rather then against this query specifically like so:

```
balances.update([{
            'amount': s_balance['amount'] + amount
        }]).where(balances.address == receiver).run()
```

This makes little sense considering that the `s_balance` object already has all of the information it needs to perform an update query baked into itself:

* columns
* values
* corresponding table

I propose a new interface that would cut down in total LOC required to update a variable that has been pulled from the DB. The interface would feel like this:

```
s_balance = balances.select().where(balances.address == sender).run()

s_balance['amount'] -= 10

s_balance.commit()
```

This interface feels better for several reasons. For one, it splits the transformational logic from the updating logic. Modification to the values inside of the variable should not exist inline or on the fly of committing the value because it could fail. Creating a new variable to store the information seems redundant. Secondly, commit() feels like it is an action running locally to the variable it is being called from which informs the developer that their actions are not modifying the entire DB, but just this variable in the DB.

### Inserting, Updating, Deleting

The same idea can cascade to the other types of database operations.

```
wallet = balances.new()
wallet.amount = 100
wallet.owner = 'stu'
wallet.commit()
```

```
wallet = balances.where(owner == 'stu')
wallet.delete()
```

### Why Change Against the Status Quo

Seneca derives its DB methods from SQLAlchemy which is probably the most popular Python database driver. This allows us to tap into a vast resource of developers that already exist and are familiar with SQLAlchemy.

However, production SQLAlchemy is generally done with their ORM interface, which is how it is utilized in the popular web framework Django. Fewer people actually use the 'raw' SQL query construction methods.

The raw SQL interface is not Pythonic and exists in its own box. It is verbose and pretty ugly. By abstracting it, even if though a simple wrapper on top of the Seneca SQL Intermediary libraries, it becomes much cleaner and pretty. :)

### Next Steps

I'm seeking feedback. Do people agree with this assessment? Is the interface that I provided optimal? Does it make sense to you as a developer?

issue_title: Schema validation error gives "Response object is too long."
issue_body:
Thanks for this library! We're using it quite heavily for our DevOps solution, and it has been working really well.

I just ran into a case where a resource failed the schema validation, but the CloudFormation console simply shows "Response object is too long." as the error message.

I think sending the entire schema where the validation failed might be too much. If we just cut off the  error message a little earlier, that would be great.

I just looked at where that message is coming from, and it appears to be a simple `str(e)` on the validation error, which resolves to [this big message](https://github.com/Julian/jsonschema/blob/master/jsonschema/exceptions.py#L59) My suggested fix would be to catch the [ValidationError](https://python-jsonschema.readthedocs.io/en/latest/errors/#jsonschema.exceptions.ValidationError) and only pass along the `message` attribute.

If that sounds good, I would be happy to whip up a PR. Thanks!

issue_title: Decoding downloaded Jpeg2000 images without saving them first
issue_body:
So far we don't know how to correctly decode Jpeg2000 images the package downloads without saving them first. Therefore for example `AwsTileRequest(...).get_data(save_data=False)` raises an error.

The problem is that original Sentinel-2 L1C images provided by ESA and stored on AWS have a nonstandard 15-bit Jpeg2000 encryption. So most of the Python packages (e.g. OpenCV, Pillow) incorrectly read these images as 16-bit data and return values that are 2 times larger as they should be. The only package we know that reads the images correctly is Glymur. However it requires a filename of stored image and does not support reading from `BytesIO`, `StringIO`, `numpy.array` or any other structure. Therefore data always needs to be first saved locally.

Does anyone know a solution for this?
(Maybe some other package we don't know about, a clever "hack" in mentioned packages or even simulating a filename path without actually saving image on disk...)

issue_title: "Check failed" in `createIndex` for sparse data containing zero rows
issue_body:
When creating an index for sparse data from the Python wrapper (calling `createIndex` with `scipy.sparse.csr_matrix`), it fails if there are zero vectors (i.e. all-zero rows) in the input data.

Here's a minimal repro-case:
```python
import numpy as np
from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix 
import nmslib 

data = csr_matrix((3, 3), dtype=np.float64)
data[0, 0] = 1
data[1, 0] = 2

# uncomment the following line to get rid of an empty row (and a crash)
#data[2, 0] = 3 

index = nmslib.init(method='hnsw', space='cosinesimil_sparse', data_type=nmslib.DataType.SPARSE_VECTOR) 
index.addDataPointBatch(data)

index.createIndex({'M': 30, 'indexThreadQty': 4, 'efConstruction': 100, 'post' : 0}) 
```

This results in:
```
Check failed: obj1->datalength() > 0
...
RuntimeError: Check failed: it's either a bug or inconsistent data!
```

Uncommenting the line `data[2, 0] = 3` in the above snippet makes it work.

Possibly some internal data structures do not get initialized when loading the data from Python (in case there are empty rows in `csr_matrix`)?

issue_title: FluentInbox issue related to fetched_count
issue_body:
This library is a huge time saver, so thanks for creating it AND for the app authorization readme. Im sure that read me saved a bit of time trying to figure out how to get authorized:)

Today I started using FluentInbox to see how current message pointer works as I have a very large inbox to process

When I do a fetch_first (or _next), I get this:

```
 File "/Users/mriffey/.local/share/virtualenvs/TestFluent.py/lib/python3.6/site-packages/O365/fluent_inbox.py", line 167, in fetch_first
    self.fetched_count = 0
AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'fetched_count'
```

Normally, this is a fairly easy issue to find, but this one has me stumped so far. The code that causes it is as follows:

```
finb = FluentInbox
finb.fetch_first(2)
finb.fetch_next(3)
for message in finb.messages:
    print(message.json['DateTimeReceived'])
```
